title: agropyron fragile var. angustifolium
intro: Agropyron fragile var. angustifolium, also known as blue wild rye, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of blue wild rye include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared for use in herbal remedies through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, typically taken as a tea or used topically to soothe skin irritations.
